web.title: Using OpenRouter With Roo Code
web.description: Configure OpenRouter in Roo Code to access 100+ language models from various providers through a single API with automatic model discovery.
web.markdown:
[Skip to main content](https://docs.roocode.com/providers/openrouter#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

OpenRouter is an AI platform that provides access to a wide variety of language models from different providers, all through a single API. This can simplify setup and allow you to easily experiment with different models.

**Website:** [https://openrouter.ai/](https://openrouter.ai/)

* * *

## Getting an API Key [​](https://docs.roocode.com/providers/openrouter\#getting-an-api-key "Direct link to Getting an API Key")

1. **Sign Up/Sign In:** Go to the [OpenRouter website](https://openrouter.ai/). Sign in with your Google or GitHub account.
2. **Get an API Key:** Go to the [keys page](https://openrouter.ai/keys). You should see an API key listed. If not, create a new key.
3. **Copy the Key:** Copy the API key.

* * *

## Available Models [​](https://docs.roocode.com/providers/openrouter\#available-models "Direct link to Available Models")

Roo Code automatically fetches all available models from OpenRouter's API (100+ models from various providers).

For the complete, up-to-date model list with pricing and capabilities, see [OpenRouter's models page](https://openrouter.ai/models).

* * *

## Configuration in Roo Code [​](https://docs.roocode.com/providers/openrouter\#configuration-in-roo-code "Direct link to Configuration in Roo Code")

1. **Open Roo Code Settings:** Click the gear icon () in the Roo Code panel.
2. **Select Provider:** Choose "OpenRouter" from the "API Provider" dropdown.
3. **Enter API Key:** Paste your OpenRouter API key into the "OpenRouter API Key" field.
4. **Select Model:** Choose your desired model from the "Model" dropdown.
5. **(Optional) Custom Base URL:** If you need to use a custom base URL for the OpenRouter API, check "Use custom base URL" and enter the URL. Leave this blank for most users.

* * *

## Tips and Notes [​](https://docs.roocode.com/providers/openrouter\#tips-and-notes "Direct link to Tips and Notes")

- **Model Selection:** OpenRouter offers a wide range of models. Experiment to find the best one for your needs.
- **Pricing:** OpenRouter charges based on the underlying model's pricing. See the [OpenRouter Models page](https://openrouter.ai/models) for details.
- **Prompt Caching:**
  - OpenRouter passes caching requests to underlying models that support it. Check the [OpenRouter Models page](https://openrouter.ai/models) to see which models offer caching.
  - For most models, caching should activate automatically if supported by the model itself (similar to how Requesty works).
  - **Models with prompt caching support include:**
    - Anthropic Claude Sonnet 3.5, 3.7
    - Anthropic Claude Haiku 3.5
    - **Anthropic Claude Haiku 4.5** (newly added)
    - Google Gemini models (with manual activation - see below)
  - **Exception for Gemini Models via OpenRouter:** Due to potential response delays sometimes observed with Google's caching mechanism when accessed via OpenRouter, a manual activation step is required _specifically for Gemini models_.
  - If using a **Gemini model** via OpenRouter, you **must manually check** the "Enable Prompt Caching" box in the provider settings to activate caching for that model. This checkbox serves as a temporary workaround. For non-Gemini models on OpenRouter, this checkbox is not necessary for caching.
- **Bring Your Own Key (BYOK):** If you use your own key for the underlying service, OpenRouter charges 5% of what it normally would. Roo Code automatically adjusts the cost calculation to reflect this.

- [Getting an API Key](https://docs.roocode.com/providers/openrouter#getting-an-api-key)
- [Available Models](https://docs.roocode.com/providers/openrouter#available-models)
- [Configuration in Roo Code](https://docs.roocode.com/providers/openrouter#configuration-in-roo-code)
- [Tips and Notes](https://docs.roocode.com/providers/openrouter#tips-and-notes)

Like most of the internet, we use cookies. Are you OK with that?

DeclineAccept

web.title: Roo Code with OpenRouter
web.description: Use Roo Code with OpenRouter to access hundreds of AI models. An AI-powered autonomous coding agent that lives in your editor.
web.markdown:
[All apps](https://openrouter.ai/works-with-openrouter)

![Roo Code with OpenRouter](https://openrouter.ai/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fawesome%2Froo-code.png&w=256&q=75)

# Roo Code

[View source code](https://github.com/RooCodeInc/Roo-Code "View source code")

coding

## About Roo Code

An AI-powered autonomous coding agent that lives in your editor.

[Visit Roo Code](https://roocode.com/) [Documentation](https://docs.roocode.com/providers/openrouter)

## Getting Started with OpenRouter for Roo Code

1. Create an account at [openrouter.ai](https://openrouter.ai/)
2. Add credits and generate an API key at [openrouter.ai/settings/keys](https://openrouter.ai/settings/keys)
3. Configure your API key in [Roo Code](https://docs.roocode.com/providers/openrouter)— see their [OpenRouter integration guide](https://docs.roocode.com/providers/openrouter) for setup details

## Using OpenRouter with Roo Code

Roo Code supports OpenRouter as a provider, giving you access to hundreds of AI models through a single API key. OpenRouter handles model routing and fallbacks so you can focus on what matters.

For detailed setup instructions and configuration options, visit the [Roo Code OpenRouter documentation](https://docs.roocode.com/providers/openrouter).

web.title: Roo Code Docs | Roo Code Documentation
web.description: Learn how to use Roo Code, the autonomous AI coding agent that helps you code faster and smarter in VS Code. Complete guides, tutorials, and documentation.
web.markdown:
[Skip to main content](https://docs.roocode.com/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Roo Code is an AI-coding suite of products designed to take full advantage of the most advanced large-language models and change how you create software at a fundamental level. It's unapologetically powerful and customizable, and it costs more to run than the alternatives because it uses frontier models with actual file system access, terminal control, and multi-step workflows.

Roo's approach is to trade tokens for quality. If you want the best and most effective AI coding experience available, this is it.

Roo Code is available in two ways:

- [Roo Code VS Code Extension](https://docs.roocode.com/getting-started/installing) works locally in your VS Code IDE and gives you full control over your coding experience. It's great for deep or highly iterative solo development work. It's highly customizable and fits into your existing workflow.
- [Roo Code Cloud Agents](https://docs.roocode.com/roo-code-cloud/cloud-agents) are an autonomous AI development team working 24/7, as a team, collaborating with you and other co-workers on a wide range of tasks. It meets you where you do work (Slack, Github, etc) and allows for large parts of software development to happen entirely away from an IDE.

The two options are highly complementary and built on the same shared foundation, achieving similarly excellent results.

## What can Roo Code do for you [​](https://docs.roocode.com/\#what-can-roo-code-do-for-you "Direct link to What can Roo Code do for you")

- Generate Code from natural language descriptions and specs
- Refactor & debug existing code
- Write & update documentation
- Answer questions about your codebase
- Automate repetitive tasks
- Utilize MCP Servers

## A mindset for success [​](https://docs.roocode.com/\#a-mindset-for-success "Direct link to A mindset for success")

The most successful Roo users tend to embrace the following ideas:

- **Leverage model agnosticism:** Roo isn't an LLM model, it needs an [LLM provider](https://docs.roocode.com/providers) to work. But it's compatible with dozens of providers and hundreds of models, so you're free to experiment, optimize and switch around, by design. No lock-ins in a world where "the best model" changes every other week.
- **Don't skimp on tokens:** expensive state-of-the-art models with lots of tokens will almost always beat cheap models using few tokens. Pay attention to your budget, but embrace the fact that tokens are cheaper than developer time.
- **Trust roles:** The extension's customizable Modes (Architect, Code, etc) and the pre-defined Cloud Agent Types (Planner, Coder, etc) keep the models on track and focused on the task at hand, without stepping outside of their boundaries. Lean on them depending on the task you're working on.
- **Be ambitious:** Roo works best the more you trust it. Out-of-the-box, the Extension will ask for your permission to everything, but as you become more comfortable and enable [Auto-Approve](https://docs.roocode.com/features/auto-approving-actions), you'll see it work for long periods on its own without getting stuck or making bad decisions. Once ready, throw large, complex projects at the [Orchestrator](https://docs.roocode.com/basic-usage/using-modes#orchestrator-mode-aka-boomerang-mode), who will coordinate tasks across Modes. In the Cloud, we handle all of this for you.

## Getting Started [​](https://docs.roocode.com/\#getting-started "Direct link to Getting Started")

- [With the Extension](https://docs.roocode.com/getting-started/installing)
- [With Roo Code Cloud](https://docs.roocode.com/roo-code-cloud/overview)

## Tutorial & Feature Videos [​](https://docs.roocode.com/\#tutorial--feature-videos "Direct link to Tutorial & Feature Videos")

[![Installing Roo Code](https://img.youtube.com/vi/Mcq3r1EPZ-4/maxresdefault.jpg)\\
Installing Roo Code](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Mcq3r1EPZ-4) [![Configuring Profiles](https://img.youtube.com/vi/ZBML8h5cCgo/maxresdefault.jpg)\\
Configuring Profiles](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZBML8h5cCgo) [![Codebase Indexing](https://img.youtube.com/vi/r1bpod1VWhg/maxresdefault.jpg)\\
Codebase Indexing](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=r1bpod1VWhg) [![Custom Modes](https://img.youtube.com/vi/iiAv1eKOaxk/maxresdefault.jpg)\\
Custom Modes](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iiAv1eKOaxk) [![Checkpoints](https://img.youtube.com/vi/Ho30nyY332E/maxresdefault.jpg)\\
Checkpoints](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ho30nyY332E) [![Todo Lists](https://img.youtube.com/vi/6h5vB9PpoPk/maxresdefault.jpg)\\
Todo Lists](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6h5vB9PpoPk)

[More quick tutorial and feature videos...](https://docs.roocode.com/tutorial-videos)

## Additional Resources [​](https://docs.roocode.com/\#additional-resources "Direct link to Additional Resources")

- [Discord Server](https://discord.gg/roocode): Join the community for real-time help and discussion.
- [Reddit Community](https://www.reddit.com/r/RooCode): Share your experiences and see what others are building.
- [YouTube Channel](https://youtube.com/@roocodeyt?feature=shared): Watch tutorials and see features in action.
- [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/RooCodeInc/Roo-Code/issues): Report bugs and track development.
- [Feature Requests](https://github.com/RooCodeInc/Roo-Code/discussions/categories/feature-requests?discussions_q=is%3Aopen+category%3A%22Feature+Requests%22+sort%3Atop): Have an idea? Share it with the team.

- [What can Roo Code do for you](https://docs.roocode.com/#what-can-roo-code-do-for-you)
- [A mindset for success](https://docs.roocode.com/#a-mindset-for-success)
- [Getting Started](https://docs.roocode.com/#getting-started)
- [Tutorial & Feature Videos](https://docs.roocode.com/#tutorial--feature-videos)
- [Additional Resources](https://docs.roocode.com/#additional-resources)
